name: Cohiba Siglo I
description: The Siglo I is the entry point of the Cohiba Siglo line, launched in 1992 as part of Cuba's prestigious Línea 1492 series. A mild, elegant smoke with the signature Cohiba triple-fermentation refinement.

name: Cohiba Siglo II
description: The Siglo II is a classic Corona from Cohiba's Línea 1492, offering a balanced, medium-bodied smoke with creamy, floral notes and the hallmark smoothness of triple-fermented Cohiba leaf.

name: Cohiba Siglo III
description: The Siglo III brings extra length to the Línea 1492, allowing the Cuban blend to develop complexity from a brisk light to a rich, earthy finish. Considered one of the finest Lonsdales in the Cohiba range.

name: Cohiba Siglo IV
description: With a larger ring gauge than the Siglo III, the Siglo IV delivers a fuller, creamier draw. Rich cedar, leather, and dark chocolate notes evolve gracefully over its 60-minute burn, placing it among Cuba's elite vitolas.

name: Cohiba Siglo V
description: The longest of the core Siglo line, the Siglo V is a masterclass in progression. Its elegant, slender profile allows the rich Cuban ligero to open up slowly, building from subtle creaminess into deep chocolate and earth.

name: Cohiba Coronas Especiales
description: An elegant, slender smoke from Cohiba's classic Línea Clásica. The Coronas Especiales is prized for its exceptional refinement—floral, creamy notes that unfold over a leisurely burn, making it a favourite of aficionados seeking grace over power.

name: Cohiba Behike BHK 54
description: The BHK 54 is the entry vitola of Cohiba's ultra-premium Behike line, incorporating rare medio tiempo leaves for unparalleled complexity. Rich, powerful, and impossibly smooth—considered one of the finest Cuban cigars ever made.

name: Cohiba Behike BHK 56
description: The crown jewel of the Cohiba Behike line, the BHK 56 is among the rarest and most sought-after Cuban cigars. Blended with medio tiempo leaves, it delivers extraordinary depth—dark chocolate, espresso, and black pepper cascading through a velvet draw.

name: Cohiba Short
description: The Cohiba Short is a quick, refined smoke from the classic Línea Clásica. Deceptively complex for its size, it opens with creamy floral notes and finishes with a gentle cedar sweetness—perfect when time is short but quality cannot be compromised.

name: Cohiba Exquisitos
description: One of the most slender vitolas in the Cohiba range, the Exquisitos delivers a delicate, aromatic smoke with pronounced floral and honey notes. The slim ring gauge concentrates the heat, amplifying the characteristic Cohiba elegance.

name: Cohiba Secretos
description: The Secretos is among the smallest vitolas in the Cohiba portfolio, yet retains every hallmark of the brand—triple-fermented leaves, immaculate construction, and a creamy, floral character that belies its modest dimensions.

name: Cohiba Panetelas
description: One of the most slender cigars produced under the Cohiba marque, the Panetelas is an exercise in delicacy. The ultra-thin ring gauge produces an intensely aromatic, grass-forward smoke with classic Cohiba creaminess.

name: Cohiba Sublime
description: A limited-edition masterpiece from Cohiba, the Sublime is a massive, long-filler Gran Corona designed for the most dedicated aficionados. Powerful and complex, it evolves from cedar and coffee notes into dark chocolate and black pepper over nearly two hours.

name: Cohiba Club
description: The Cohiba Club is a petite cigarillo—machine-made but using high-quality Cohiba leaf. It captures the brand's creamy elegance in a short, convenient format ideal for a 20-minute break or as an after-dinner smoke when time is limited.

name: Cohiba Robustos Supremos
description: An exclusive limited edition released to Cohiba's La Casa del Habano network, the Robustos Supremos packs an extraordinary ring gauge into a Robusto frame. Rich, dense, and full-bodied—a cigar that demands full attention and rewards with complex dark chocolate and espresso.

name: Cohiba Coronas Edición Limitada
description: Released as part of Habanos S.A.'s prestigious Edición Limitada programme, this Cohiba Corona is crafted from aged, specially selected leaves. The limited production ensures each cigar is exceptional—dried fruit, cedar, and subtle spice layered over a creamy base.

name: Cohiba Añejados
description: Part of the Habanos Añejados range, these Cohibas are rolled and then aged for a further five years before release. The extended ageing rounds every edge—spice gives way to cocoa, cedar softens, and dried fruit emerges, producing a supple, supremely harmonious smoke.

name: Cohiba Gran Reserva
description: The Cohiba Gran Reserva uses tobaccos that have been aged for a minimum of three years after harvest before rolling, then further rested in the factory. The result is a masterpiece of harmony—rich, complex, and remarkably smooth for its medium-full strength.

name: Partagás Serie D No.1
description: A robust, full-flavoured Cuban from the legendary Serie D family. The No.1 packs a potent blend of leather, dark chocolate, and peppery earth into a shorter, wider vitola—delivering the signature Partagás intensity in a satisfying, medium-length smoke.

name: Partagás Serie D No.2
description: The Serie D No.2 brings the iconic Partagás Serie D blend into a tapered Piramide format. The closed head concentrates flavours at the cold draw and gradually opens into a powerful, pepper-forward experience that deepens into leather and espresso.

name: Partagás Serie D No.5
description: The Serie D No.5 is a shorter, slightly narrower take on the legendary Partagás Serie D blend. Immensely popular for its full-bodied intensity compressed into a sub-45-minute format—intense pepper, leather, and coffee hitting from the first puff.

name: Partagás Serie E No.2
description: The Serie E No.2 is Partagás at its most imposing—a Prominente of extraordinary dimensions that allows the full Serie E blend to unfold over 80 minutes. Waves of leather, earth, and dark chocolate build to a crescendo of pepper and espresso.

name: Partagás Aristocratas
description: The Partagás Aristocratas is a classic Cuban Lonsdale—elegant, tapered, and full of character. The length gives room for the rich Partagás ligero blend to evolve gracefully, moving from bright cedar into deep leather and pepper.

name: Partagás Presidente
description: The Partagás Presidente is among the most celebrated Double Coronas in all of Cuba. Powerful and long-burning, it rewards patient smokers with waves of leather, chocolate, and pepper that build relentlessly across its remarkable 90-minute journey.

name: Partagás Coronas Gordas
description: A wider-gauge take on the classic Corona format, the Partagás Coronas Gordas delivers the full Partagás intensity in a shorter, chubbier vitola. Rich and earthy with deep leather and coffee notes throughout its hour-long burn.

name: Partagás Salomones
description: A limited-edition giant from Partagás, the Salomones is an imposing gran corona that showcases the brand's most powerful blend at its absolute maximum. Rich, leather-forward, with cascading waves of dark chocolate, espresso, and brutal pepper.

name: Partagás Coronas Junior
description: The Partagás Coronas Junior is a compact, entry-level Cuban that retains the character of the broader Partagás portfolio. A quick 30-minute smoke with cedar, leather, and a light pepper bite—accessible and well-made for the price.

name: Partagás Charlotte
description: The Partagás Charlotte is a slim, accessible vitola that offers a lighter introduction to the Partagás profile. Cedar and earth dominate, with a gentle leather-coffee backbone and just enough pepper to confirm its Cuban identity.

name: Partagás Super Partagás
description: The Super Partagás lives up to its name—an oversized Robusto with a commanding presence and a powerful, full-bodied blend. Cedar, leather, and strong earthy notes ride the entire smoke, underpinned by consistent spice.

name: Partagás Churchills
description: The Partagás Churchills is a classic Cuban Churchill—big, bold, and uncompromising. Over 85 minutes, the rich Partagás blend moves from cedar and leather into deep dark chocolate and coffee, with a persistent pepper finish that defines its Cuban character.

name: Partagás Habaneros
description: The Partagás Habaneros is a well-balanced Corona-sized Cuban with a medium-full character. Cedar and coffee open the smoke, with a nutty, earthy backbone that keeps the more aggressive Partagás intensity at bay—a great everyday smoke.

name: Partagás Serie P No.1
description: The Serie P No.1 is the powerful counterpart to the Serie D, housed in a Piramide format. The tapered head delivers a concentrated, intense first draw that gradually opens into a complex, full-bodied experience of leather, dark chocolate, and black pepper.

name: Partagás Línea Maestros Churchill
description: Part of the exclusive Línea Maestros series, this Churchill is crafted by Partagás's most experienced rollers using only the finest aged leaves. The extra care shows—richer, more complex, and more harmonious than the standard Churchills, with dried fruit nuances emerging late.

name: Partagás Culebras
description: One of the rarest presentations in the Habanos S.A. portfolio, the Partagás Culebras consists of three Panetelas braided together and sold as a single unit. A collector's item as much as a smoke—unbraided before lighting, each stick delivers the true Partagás character.

name: Partagás Ramón Allones 8
description: The Ramón Allones 8 is the most celebrated vitola of the Ramón Allones brand (distributed under Partagás ownership). Rich and earthy with great complexity, it punches above its weight—an accessible full-bodied Robusto beloved by Cuban cigar veterans.

name: Bolívar Coronas Gigantes
description: The Coronas Gigantes is the flagship vitola of the Bolívar brand—a massive Double Corona that is among the most powerful and respected Cuban cigars. Dense, earthy, and uncompromising, it builds to a crescendo of black pepper and dark chocolate.

name: Bolívar Palmas Extra
description: A slim, medium-full Panetela from Bolívar's classic range. The Palmas Extra delivers the brand's earthy, leathery character in a format that burns long and cool—an underrated choice for lovers of Cuban character who prefer a thinner ring gauge.

name: Bolívar Gold Medal
description: Named to honour the brand's many international accolades, the Bolívar Gold Medal is a powerful, full-bodied Cuban in a long Corona format. Aggressive pepper and earth are anchored by dark chocolate and coffee, making it a benchmark for full-strength Cuban smoking.

name: Bolívar Inmensas
description: The Bolívar Inmensas is a long, elegant Laguito format that allows the brand's heavy, full-bodied blend to breathe and develop gradually. Waves of earth and leather are joined by dark chocolate and coffee that deepen into a memorable, pepper-laced finish.

name: Bolívar Tubos No.3
description: The Bolívar Tubos No.3 is sold in an individual aluminium tube, making it one of the most practical and travel-friendly Cuban cigars available. The Corona-sized smoke delivers classic Bolívar earth and leather with cedar and gentle pepper—an accessible entry to the brand.

name: Bolívar Coronas Extra
description: A slightly larger Corona with extra ring gauge, the Bolívar Coronas Extra is one of the most popular everyday Bolívars. The added girth intensifies the flavour delivery—strong leather and earth punctuated by consistent pepper and coffee throughout.

name: Bolívar Petit Bouquets
description: The Bolívar Petit Bouquets is a small, accessible Cuban that offers a taste of the Bolívar character at a lower entry point. A 30-minute smoke that hints at the earthiness and leather of the bigger vitolas without their full-throttle power.

name: Bolívar Regentes
description: The Bolívar Regentes is a tapered Taco-format cigar that combines the brand's characteristic earthiness and leather with slightly mellower transitions courtesy of its closed foot. A well-regarded vitola among aficionados seeking a classic Bolívar experience.

name: Bolívar Champagnes
description: The smallest vitola in the standard Bolívar range, the Champagnes is a quick, slender smoke that captures the brand's intensity in miniature. Cedar and light earth with a peppery kick make it a compact, economical way to enjoy a Cuban after a meal.

name: Bolívar Short Churchills
description: The Short Churchills packages the essence of a Bolívar Churchill into a Robusto-length vitola. The broader ring gauge intensifies every note—aggressive leather, earth, and dark chocolate that give way to a powerful pepper finish over 55 minutes.

name: Bolívar Coronas
description: The classic Bolívar Corona is the standard-bearer of the line—a no-nonsense, full-bodied Cuban with earth, leather, and cedar that rarely disappoints. An excellent introduction to the Bolívar brand for smokers graduating from milder profiles.

name: Bolívar Royal Coronas Edición Limitada
description: The Edición Limitada version of the Royal Coronas uses specially selected, fully aged Vuelta Abajo leaves for a noticeably richer and more complex smoke. The tobacco's additional years of maturation smooth the edges of Bolívar's signature aggression, revealing concentrated espresso and dark chocolate notes.

name: Bolívar Poderosos
description: The Poderosos—Spanish for 'Powerful'—lives up to its name with one of the widest ring gauges in the standard Bolívar portfolio. A dense, aggressive smoke packed with leather, earth, and black pepper that only the most experienced aficionados should tackle straight.

name: Bolívar Gold Medal Reserva
description: A limited Gran Reserva release using specially aged Vuelta Abajo leaf, the Gold Medal Reserva elevates the standard Gold Medal blend to a new level of complexity. Dried fruit and dark chocolate take centre stage, supported by Bolívar's trademark leather and earth.

name: Bolívar Inmensas Edición Regional
description: A regional edition of the classic Inmensas vitola, produced for a specific Habanos S.A. market. The tobaccos are drawn from a particularly strong vintage, amplifying the Bolívar character—richer chocolate, more aggressive pepper, and a long, complex finish.
